name: US Heatwave CDD Spike → Data Center Cooling Cost → Cloud Margin Compression
thesis: Extreme summer heat spikes cooling costs for data centers (40% of DC energy is cooling). When NOAA CDD (Cooling Degree Days) spike >2σ in key DC corridors (Northern Virginia, Dallas, Phoenix), colocation REITs face margin compression while hyperscalers with owned facilities and liquid cooling absorb it. Short DLR/EQIX, long GOOGL/MSFT during extreme heat events.
causal_chain: NOAA CPC forecasts above-normal summer heat for DC corridors
CDD spike >2σ above 10yr avg in Ashburn VA / Dallas TX / Phoenix AZ
Data center PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) degrades — cooling draws 40%+ of total power
Colocation REITs (DLR, EQIX) face higher electricity bills on pass-through contracts with caps
Hyperscalers with custom liquid cooling (Google, Microsoft) absorb with lower PUE impact
DLR/EQIX operating margins compress 50-100bps in Q3 earnings
source_reference: Uptime Institute PUE surveys; DOE data center energy reports

name: USDA Cattle on Feed Report Surprise → Feeder Cattle Futures
thesis: USDA publishes monthly Cattle on Feed report (free, 3pm ET on reporting day). When placements exceed analyst estimates by >5%, feeder cattle futures (GF=F) drop 2-5% over the next 5 trading days as the market prices in future supply glut. When placements miss by >5%, GF=F rallies. The report is released after market close — overnight futures gap is the entry.
causal_chain: USDA Cattle on Feed report published monthly (free at usda.library.cornell.edu)
Placements figure exceeds/misses pre-report estimates (CME/Reuters consensus)
Higher placements = more cattle entering feedlots = future supply increase in 4-6 months
Feeder cattle futures (GF=F) gap down overnight / rally on miss
Full repricing takes 3-5 trading days as cash market adjusts
data_sources: CME/Reuters consensus estimates (free via trade press)
source_reference: USDA NASS Cattle on Feed; Livestock Marketing Information Center

name: China PMI Manufacturing → Copper/Iron Ore 5-Day Momentum
thesis: China NBS Manufacturing PMI is released on the last day of each month (or 1st of next month). When PMI crosses above 50 (expansion) after being below 50 for 2+ months, industrial metals (copper HG=F, iron ore via BHP/VALE) rally 3-8% over the next 5-10 trading days as global commodity desks reprice China demand. The signal is the expansion threshold crossing, not the absolute level.
causal_chain: China NBS releases monthly Manufacturing PMI (free at stats.gov.cn, 1st of month)
PMI crosses above 50 after 2+ months below (expansion regime shift)
Global commodity trading desks reprice China industrial demand expectations
Copper (HG=F) and iron ore proxies (BHP, VALE, RIO) rally 3-8% over 5-10 days
Base metals ETFs (COPX, PICK) follow with 1-2 day lag
source_reference: China NBS; multiple commodity research desks

name: USDA Food Recall Severity → Short Protein Processor
thesis: FDA/USDA FSIS publishes food recalls in real-time (free RSS). When a Class I recall (reasonable probability of serious health consequences) hits a publicly traded protein processor (TSN, PPC, JBSAY, SAFM acquirer), the stock drops 3-8% over 5 days as distribution partners pull product and media amplifies. The recall RSS feed leads Bloomberg/Reuters by 2-6 hours.
causal_chain: USDA FSIS or FDA issues Class I recall on specific company product (free RSS at fsis.usda.gov/recalls)
Recalled product volume and distribution scope determine severity (nationwide vs regional)
Retailers (WMT, KR, COST) pull product from shelves within 24-48 hours
Media coverage amplifies consumer fear — protein processor brand takes reputational hit
Stock drops 3-8% over 5 trading days; recovery takes 2-4 weeks as replacement product ships
source_reference: USDA FSIS; FDA recall database; historical E. coli/Listeria events at Tyson/JBS

name: US Housing Permits-to-Completions Ratio → Construction Aggregate Demand
thesis: Census Bureau publishes monthly housing permits AND completions (free). When permits-to-completions ratio rises >1.3 (backlog building), it signals 6-12 months of guaranteed aggregate/cement/concrete demand. Long construction materials (VMC Vulcan, MLM Martin Marietta, EXP Eagle Materials) which have local monopoly pricing power and benefit from volume growth with operating leverage.
causal_chain: Census Bureau publishes monthly permits and completions (free at census.gov/construction)
Permits-to-completions ratio rises above 1.3 — more homes approved than finished = backlog building
Each permitted home requires 100-400 tons of aggregate, 10-30 cubic yards of concrete
Aggregate/cement producers (VMC, MLM, EXP) have local monopoly pricing (trucking radius limits competition)
Volume growth + pricing power = operating leverage — margins expand 200-400bps over next 2-3 quarters
Stocks re-rate as quarterly earnings show both volume and ASP growth
data_sources: Census Bureau PERMITS/COMPLETIONS (free monthly, also on FRED)
source_reference: Census Bureau Construction; Vulcan Materials investor presentations on local monopoly dynamics

name: CDC Wastewater Viral Surveillance → Pharma Demand Anticipation
thesis: CDC NWSS (National Wastewater Surveillance System) publishes weekly viral concentration data for COVID, RSV, influenza, and norovirus across 1000+ sites (free). Wastewater data leads clinical case counts by 1-2 weeks. When wastewater viral load for flu/RSV spikes >2σ above seasonal baseline, long diagnostic/treatment companies (MRNA flu, AZN RSV Beyfortus, DGX/LH testing volume) before the clinical surge hits hospital systems and media coverage drives demand.
causal_chain: CDC NWSS publishes weekly wastewater viral concentrations (free at cdc.gov/nwss)
Wastewater flu/RSV/COVID levels spike >2σ above seasonal baseline — leads clinical cases by 7-14 days
Diagnostic labs (DGX, LH) see testing volume surge 1-2 weeks after wastewater signal
Treatment/vaccine companies (MRNA flu booster, AZN Beyfortus RSV, GILD Tamiflu) see prescription demand surge
Media covers rising cases → consumer demand for testing/treatment amplifies the move
Pharma stocks rally 5-15% over the 4-6 week viral wave before plateauing
data_sources: yfinance MRNA, AZN, DGX, LH, GILD
source_reference: CDC NWSS; Biobot Analytics; Nature Medicine wastewater surveillance studies

name: Jet Fuel Crack Spread Spike → Airline Margin Compression
thesis: Airlines hedge fuel 6-18 months forward but imperfectly. When jet fuel crack spread (kerosene vs crude) spikes >$5/bbl above 2yr avg, unhedged fuel exposure compresses airline operating margins 1-2 quarters later. Short UAL/DAL/LUV vs long XLE as a pair.
causal_chain: Refinery turnaround season or geopolitical shock spikes distillate cracks
Jet fuel crack (kerosene vs WTI) widens >$5/bbl above 2yr rolling average
Airlines hedged at lower crack levels face margin compression on unhedged portion (typically 30-50%)
Fuel cost surprise flows through to Q+1 and Q+2 earnings as hedges roll off
Airline stocks underperform energy stocks for 1-3 months
data_sources: yfinance UAL, DAL, LUV, XLE, SPY
source_reference: EIA petroleum data; airline 10-Q fuel hedge disclosures

name: BNPL Delinquency Spike → Short Affirm/Consumer Credit
thesis: CFPB publishes BNPL market reports showing delinquency rates (free). When BNPL 30+ DPD rises >100bps QoQ, it signals deteriorating consumer credit quality among young borrowers before it shows up in traditional bank NIM compression. Short AFRM, long traditional banks (JPM/WFC) as a spread.
causal_chain: CFPB BNPL market report shows 30+ DPD rising >100bps QoQ
BNPL borrowers are younger, lower-FICO — canary in the coal mine for broader consumer stress
AFRM provision expense spikes 1-2 quarters after DPD rises as losses crystallize
Traditional banks with tighter underwriting see relative outperformance
Long JPM/WFC vs short AFRM captures the credit-quality divergence
source_reference: CFPB BNPL market report 2024; NY Fed consumer credit panel

name: US Scrap Steel Price → Long Steel Mills on Margin Expansion
thesis: US EAF (electric arc furnace) steel mills use scrap as primary input (~70% of US steel production). When scrap prices drop >15% from peak while finished steel HRC prices stay elevated (spread widens), steel mill margins expand dramatically. Long NUE/STLD (EAF mills) for 3-6 months. AMM scrap prices are partially free via trade press.
causal_chain: Scrap steel prices decline >15% from trailing 6mo peak (tracked by AMM/Fastmarkets, partially free)
Finished HRC steel prices remain elevated due to demand/trade protection (FRED WPUSI019011)
HRC-scrap spread widens beyond $400/ton (vs avg ~$300)
EAF mills (NUE, STLD) see raw material cost drop while selling price holds → margin expansion
Earnings beat consensus 1-2 quarters later as the spread flows through to P&L
source_reference: AMM scrap index; Nucor/Steel Dynamics 10-Q metal margin disclosures

name: Class 8 Truck Orders Collapse → Freight Capacity Shortage 18mo Later
thesis: ACT Research publishes monthly Class 8 truck net orders (free summary via trade press). When orders collapse >40% YoY for 3+ consecutive months, it signals carriers are not replacing aging fleet. 18 months later, the average fleet age rises past replacement threshold, capacity tightens, and truckload rates spike. Long PCAR (Paccar, Class 8 OEM) 12-18mo after order trough — replacement cycle demand is deferred, not destroyed.
causal_chain: ACT Research reports Class 8 net orders collapse >40% YoY for 3+ months
Carriers defer fleet replacement during freight recession (average truck is 7+ years old)
12-18 months later: aging fleet hits DOT compliance limits, maintenance costs spike
Forced replacement cycle begins — Class 8 orders surge from trough
PCAR/CMI (Cummins engines) see order book rebuild → revenue acceleration → stock re-rates
data_sources: ACT Research monthly (free summary via FreightWaves/trade press)
FRED IPMAN (manufacturing IP as cycle proxy)
source_reference: ACT Research; FTR Transportation Intelligence; DOT FMCSA age-of-fleet reports

name: US Rental Vacancy Rate Trough → Long Apartment REITs
thesis: Census Bureau publishes quarterly rental vacancy rate (free, FRED RRVRUSQ156N). When vacancy hits a cyclical trough (<5.5%) and starts rising, it paradoxically signals PEAK RENT GROWTH is about to hit REIT earnings — because the vacancy trough means landlords had maximum pricing power for the prior 2-3 quarters, and those rents flow through to earnings with a 1-2 quarter lag. Long apartment REITs (EQR, AVB, MAA) when vacancy bottoms.
causal_chain: Census Bureau rental vacancy rate hits trough (below 5.5%) — published quarterly on FRED
Trough vacancy = landlords had maximum pricing power for prior 6-12 months
Lease renewals at peak rents flow through to REIT same-store NOI with 1-2 quarter lag
Apartment REITs (EQR, AVB, MAA) report earnings beats as peak rents hit financials
Stock re-rates on NOI acceleration even as vacancy starts rising (the market watches vacancy, not the lagged rent)
data_sources: yfinance EQR, AVB, MAA, VNQ, SPY
source_reference: Census Bureau Housing Vacancies; NMHC apartment market surveys

name: Baltic Dry Index Spike → Dry Bulk Shipper Earnings Surprise → Long SBLK/GOGL
thesis: The Baltic Dry Index (BDI) is a real-time measure of shipping rates for dry bulk commodities (iron ore, coal, grain). When BDI spikes >50% from 3-month low, dry bulk shipper spot earnings jump but stock prices lag by 2-4 weeks because sell-side models use contract-rate assumptions, not spot. Long SBLK/GOGL (spot-exposed shippers) for 60 days after BDI spike.
causal_chain: BDI spikes >50% from trailing 3-month low (tracked daily, free via FRED DBDI or yfinance BDIY)
Spot charter rates for Capesize/Panamax vessels surge — daily TCE revenue doubles
Spot-exposed shippers (SBLK, GOGL) earn windfall rates on open vessels immediately
Sell-side models update with 2-4 week lag (use contract/average rates, not spot)
Earnings surprise materializes at next quarterly report → stock re-rates
data_sources: FRED DBDI or Baltic Exchange (free summary)
yfinance SBLK, GOGL, BDRY (dry bulk ETF), SPY
source_reference: Baltic Exchange; Clarksons shipping research

name: US Initial Jobless Claims 4-Week Acceleration → Recession Hedge Pair
thesis: FRED ICSA (weekly initial claims) is the fastest official labor market indicator. When the 4-week MA of claims rises >15% from its trailing 26-week low AND the level crosses 250k, it signals labor market deterioration 2-3 months before NBER recession dating. Long TLT + short XLY (cyclical consumer) as a recession hedge pair. The signal has led every recession since 1970.
causal_chain: FRED ICSA 4-week MA rises >15% from 26-week trailing low AND crosses 250k
Labor market weakening confirmed before payrolls data (claims = leading, payrolls = lagging)
Consumer spending expectations deteriorate → cyclical discretionary (XLY) sells off
Fed pivots dovish → long-duration bonds (TLT) rally on rate-cut expectations
Pair (long TLT / short XLY) captures both legs of the recession trade
source_reference: Sahm Rule adjacent; FRED initial claims; NBER recession dating

name: USDA Crop Export Inspections Weekly Surprise → Grain Futures
thesis: USDA publishes weekly Export Inspections every Monday (free). When actual inspections exceed the prior 4-week average by >20% for corn or soybeans, it signals unexpected demand (often from China). Grain futures rally 1-3% over the next 5 trading days as the market adjusts supply/demand balance sheets. The Monday publication leads the Thursday Export Sales report by 3 days.
causal_chain: USDA Export Inspections published Monday 11am ET (free at usda.library.cornell.edu)
Weekly inspections for corn/soybeans exceed 4-week MA by >20%
Unexpected demand signal — often China buying in size
Grain futures (ZC=F, ZS=F) rally 1-3% over next 5 trading days
Thursday Export Sales report confirms the trend with 3-day lag
source_reference: USDA FAS Export Inspections; USDA Export Sales reports

name: NASA SMAP Soil Moisture Deficit -> Winter Wheat Abandonment -> KC Wheat Futures
thesis: NASA SMAP provides daily soil moisture at 9km resolution (free). When topsoil moisture in the HRW wheat belt (KS, OK, TX panhandle) falls below the 10th percentile during Oct-Mar dormancy, winter wheat abandonment spikes to 20-35% (vs 10% normal). This supply destruction is not priced until the May WASDE. Long KC wheat (KE=F) on early soil moisture signal, 2-3 months before WASDE catches up.
causal_chain: NASA SMAP satellite soil moisture shows HRW wheat belt below 10th percentile (free daily via nsidc.org)
USDA Crop Progress weekly condition ratings confirm poor/very poor rising above 40%
Winter wheat fails to establish root systems under prolonged dormancy moisture stress
Spring green-up reveals extensive winterkill - abandonment 20-35% vs 10% normal
May WASDE slashes US HRW production estimate by 100-200M bushels
KC wheat futures rally 15-30% as importers scramble for alternative origins
data_sources: NASA SMAP soil moisture (free daily via nsidc.org)
source_reference: NASA SMAP; USDA NASS Crop Progress; Kansas Wheat Commission abandonment data

name: Great Lakes Ice Cover -> Soo Locks Delay -> Blast Furnace Steel Mill Squeeze
thesis: NOAA GLERL publishes daily Great Lakes ice cover (free). When peak ice exceeds 60% (~1 in 3 winters), Soo Locks opening delays 2-4 weeks. This delays 70% of US iron ore pellet shipments from MN/MI to Gary/Indiana Harbor mills. Blast furnace operators (X, CLF) face pellet shortage while EAF mills (NUE, STLD) on scrap are unaffected. Long NUE/STLD, short X during heavy ice years.
causal_chain: NOAA GLERL daily ice analysis shows peak ice cover above 60% (free at glerl.noaa.gov)
US Army Corps delays Soo Locks opening 2-4 weeks beyond normal March 25
Iron ore pellet shipments from Mesabi/Marquette ranges delayed
Blast furnace mills (X, CLF legacy) face pellet drawdown and spot premium spikes
EAF mini-mills (NUE, STLD) run on scrap - no lake shipping dependency
HRC firms as BF production curtailed; NUE/STLD gain relative margin advantage
data_sources: NOAA GLERL Great Lakes ice cover (free daily)
source_reference: NOAA GLERL; Lake Carriers Association; USACE Soo Locks data

name: Gulf of Mexico Pre-Season SST -> Hurricane Crude Shut-in -> Crack Spread Spike
thesis: NOAA daily Gulf SST maps (free). When June SSTs in Main Development Region exceed 27.5C (80th pctile), hurricane season produces 40% more major storms. Each Cat 3+ Gulf hurricane shuts 1-2M bpd offshore production. Long 3-2-1 crack spread when pre-season SSTs signal hyperactive year, before individual storms form.
causal_chain: NOAA daily SST shows Gulf MDR above 27.5C by June (free at ospo.noaa.gov)
CSU/NOAA outlooks confirm above-average season
Cat 3+ hurricane enters Gulf - platforms evacuate, 1-2M bpd shut-in
Crude tightens regionally but refinery demand persists from pipeline/SPR
Gasoline/distillate crack spreads spike 30-60%
Long 3-2-1 crack spread for 2-6 weeks post-storm
source_reference: NOAA OSPO SST; BSEE shut-in archives; CSU tropical meteorology

name: EPA ECHO Consent Decree Pipeline -> Environmental Services Backlog Growth
thesis: EPA ECHO database updates weekly with consent decrees (free API). When rolling 12-month consent decree dollar value exceeds 2 sigma above trend, mandated remediation flows to environmental services firms (ECL, RSG, WM). Court-ordered timelines create non-discretionary capex. Long environmental services basket on decree surge.
causal_chain: EPA ECHO database shows consent decree surge (free at echo.epa.gov)
Major decrees mandate >M remediation at industrial facilities
Court-ordered timelines create non-discretionary spending regardless of cycle
ECL, RSG, WM win remediation contracts
Backlog growth visible 1-2 quarters after decree publication
Long ECL/RSG when 12-month decree value exceeds 2 sigma above trend
source_reference: EPA ECHO database; DOJ ENRD consent decree archives
reject_reason: EPA ECHO consent decree dollar amounts require complex API parsing; not tractable for automated backtest

name: USITC Section 337 Preliminary Determination -> Import Exclusion -> Long Complainant
thesis: USITC publishes Section 337 dockets on patent infringement of imports (free). ALJ preliminary determination of violation has 70%+ probability of final exclusion order banning imports. Domestic patent holder gains import monopoly. Long complainant on preliminary determination - market underprices exclusion probability.
causal_chain: USITC publishes ALJ preliminary determinations (free at usitc.gov/EDIS)
Preliminary violation signals 70%+ probability of final exclusion order
Exclusion order bans infringing imports at border - CBP enforces
Domestic complainant gains monopoly pricing in US market
Revenue uplift 6-12 months post-exclusion as import channel dries up
Long complainant stock - market underprices exclusion probability
source_reference: USITC Section 337 docket; historical exclusion order conversion rates
reject_reason: ITC Section 337 events too rare and complainant tickers vary; not systematically backtestable

name: NHTSA Engineering Analysis -> Auto OEM Warranty Reserve Shortfall
thesis: NHTSA publishes recall investigations in real-time (free). When NHTSA opens an Engineering Analysis on a high-volume model (>500K units), the OEM faces M-B warranty costs not yet accrued. EA opening is public but market discounts slowly. Short OEM on EA opening; warranty charge hits 1-3 quarters later.
causal_chain: NHTSA opens Engineering Analysis on high-volume model (free at nhtsa.gov)
EA status means defect is safety-critical - recall likely within 6-12 months
Affected population >500K implies recall cost M-B
OEM warranty reserve inadequate for EA-scale recall
Warranty accrual charge compresses EPS 1-3 quarters after recall order
Short OEM on EA opening; cover when cost fully accrued
source_reference: NHTSA Office of Defects Investigation; auto OEM 10-K warranty disclosures

name: USDA Cold Storage Poultry Glut -> Processor Liquidation -> Short TSN/PPC
thesis: USDA Cold Storage report (free monthly) shows frozen protein inventories. When frozen poultry exceeds 5-year average by >15%, processors face mounting storage costs (/bin/zsh.02-0.04/lb/month) forcing liquidation. Spot chicken prices fall 10-20% over 4-8 weeks. Short TSN (33% chicken) and PPC (80% chicken) on extreme builds.
causal_chain: USDA Cold Storage shows frozen poultry >15% above 5-year average (free monthly)
High inventories signal overproduction; storage costs compound pressure
Processors liquidate at discounted spot to reduce carrying costs
USDA AMS poultry spot prices decline 10-20% over 4-8 weeks
TSN (~33% chicken) and PPC (~80% chicken) see margin compression
Short TSN/PPC; cover when inventories normalize
source_reference: USDA NASS Cold Storage; Urner Barry poultry indices

name: China PE Export Surge -> Global Polyethylene Margin Compression -> Short LYB/DOW
thesis: China Customs monthly trade data (free). When China PE exports rise >30% YoY for 2+ months, new coal-to-olefins capacity is dumping into global markets. US PE producers (LYB, DOW, WLK) face -100/ton benchmark erosion as Chinese volumes suppress Asian prices that arbitrage to US Gulf Coast. Short LYB/DOW on confirmed surge.
causal_chain: China Customs shows PE exports >30% YoY for 2+ months (free at customs.gov.cn)
New CTO capacity in interior China reaches full production
Chinese PE floods SE Asian, Middle Eastern, US Gulf Coast markets
US PE contract prices decline -100/ton over 2-3 months
LYB, DOW, WLK face integrated margin compression
EBITDA downgrades 10-15% drive 10-20% stock declines
source_reference: GACC trade data; ICIS Asia PE; Wood Mackenzie CTO tracker
reject_reason: China GAC PE export data not free/machine-readable

name: SEMI Fab Equipment B2B Inflection -> Specialty Chemical Demand Lead
thesis: SEMI monthly semiconductor equipment book-to-bill (free summary). When B2B crosses above 1.05 from downcycle trough (<0.95 for 3+ months), fab utilization recovery drives consumable chemical demand (photoresists, CMP slurries, etch gases) within 2-4 quarters. ENTG/CCMP have 80%+ revenue tied to wafer starts. Long on inflection.
causal_chain: SEMI monthly B2B crosses above 1.05 from trough below 0.95 (free press release)
Equipment delivery signals fab reactivation within 6-12 months
Wafer starts ramp as tools installed and qualified
Each wafer pass requires photoresist, CMP slurry, etch gases - linear with starts
ENTG, CCMP see volume recovery 2-4 quarters after inflection
Revenue acceleration re-rates from trough 18-22x to 25-30x forward
source_reference: SEMI Industry Statistics; Entegris/CMC investor presentations

name: TikTok Viral Food Trend -> Ingredient Commodity Spike -> Restaurant Margin Squeeze
thesis: When a food goes viral on TikTok (>50M views/week), consumer demand spikes 200-500% within 2-4 weeks. Supply chain cannot adjust. Google Trends food-specific surges >400% WoW proxy TikTok virality. Long the underlying commodity (cottage cheese 2023, Dubai chocolate 2024). Supply chain lag creates 2-6 week tradeable window.
causal_chain: TikTok food trend crosses 50M views - confirmed via Google Trends >400% WoW
Consumer demand at grocery/restaurants spikes 200-500% within 2-4 weeks
Commodity supply chain has 4-8 week lead time - cannot scale instantly
Spot ingredient prices spike 30-80% (cottage cheese +40% 2023, feta +60% 2021)
Restaurants with fixed menus face input cost squeeze on that ingredient
Demand normalizes 8-16 weeks as trend fades - exit before reversion
source_reference: Google Trends; viral food case studies; USDA ERS demand elasticity
reject_reason: TikTok viral food trends not systematically backtestable; Google Trends data is noisy and retrospective

name: CFPB Complaint Surge by Company -> Fintech Enforcement Prediction
thesis: CFPB Complaint Database updates daily (free, 500K+/year). When company-specific complaints spike >3 sigma for 2+ months, it signals consumer harm triggering enforcement within 6-18 months. 65%+ hit rate historically. Short fintech/bank on complaint surge - market does not systematically monitor this free database.
causal_chain: CFPB Complaint DB shows company complaints >3 sigma for 2+ months (free daily)
Surge indicates systematic consumer harm (unauthorized fees, deceptive practices)
CFPB opens investigation (confidential, but complaints are leading)
Enforcement action announced 6-18 months later (consent order, fine)
Stock drops 10-25% on enforcement plus compliance costs
Short on complaint surge; catalyst within 6-18 month window
source_reference: CFPB Complaint Database; academic complaint-enforcement literature

name: Lichess Concurrent Users Spike -> Stay-at-Home Economy Proxy -> Long NFLX/RBLX
thesis: Lichess publishes real-time concurrent player counts (free API). When players spike >2 sigma above seasonal baseline (outside tournaments), it proxies macro stay-at-home shift (weather, illness, economic pullback). Leads quarterly engagement for NFLX, RBLX, TTWO, SPOT. Long stay-at-home basket on sustained chess surge.
causal_chain: Lichess API shows concurrent players >2 sigma above baseline for 5+ days (free)
Surge reflects broad stay-at-home shift (weather, illness, economic pullback)
Stay-at-home beneficiaries see engagement spike concurrently
NFLX, RBLX, TTWO, SPOT beat quarterly engagement consensus
Long stay-at-home basket; hold through next earnings cycle
source_reference: Lichess open API; COVID-era online activity correlations
reject_reason: Lichess concurrent users not a reliable macro proxy; correlation with stay-at-home is spurious post-2021

name: FCC BDC Starlink Coverage -> Rural ISP Subscriber Churn -> Short LUMN/CNSL
thesis: FCC Broadband Data Collection (quarterly, free) shows Starlink coverage by county. When Starlink median speed exceeds 100 Mbps in counties served only by 10-25 Mbps DSL, rural ISPs (LUMN, CNSL) face 15-30% subscriber churn over 12-18 months. FCC data leads earnings by 2-3 quarters. Short rural ISPs when Starlink crosses 50% of their territory.
causal_chain: FCC BDC shows Starlink expanding in rural counties at 100+ Mbps (free quarterly)
Starlink 5-10x speed advantage over incumbent DSL/fixed wireless
Rural subscribers switch - 15-30% churn over 12-18 months
LUMN rural and CNSL report subscriber losses with 2-3 quarter lag
Fixed-cost network means losses drop directly to bottom line
Short rural ISPs when Starlink crosses 50% of service territory
source_reference: FCC BDC; Ookla; SpaceX FCC filings
reject_reason: bt_feasibility 3 too low; pipeline winners all have bt>=4 with FRED/yfinance data

name: EV DCFC Utilization Threshold -> Grid Upgrade Capex -> Transformer OEM Backlog
thesis: DOE AFDC EV charger data and OCPP utilization reports. When DCFC utilization crosses 70% peak hours in a metro (vs 30% avg), it triggers utility distribution upgrade applications within 3-6 months. Each DCFC cluster needs 2-5MW. Long distribution utilities (PPL, ES) and transformer OEMs (ETN, HUBB) on utilization threshold crossing.
causal_chain: DOE AFDC/OCPP shows DCFC utilization crossing 70% in metro clusters
High utilization signals EV adoption inflection - more capacity needed
Each DCFC hub requires 2-5MW distribution upgrade to local utility
Utility files rate case for distribution capex at regulated return
Transformer OEMs (ETN, HUBB) see backlog growth from utility procurement
Long utility plus transformer OEM basket on threshold crossing
source_reference: DOE AFDC; RMI DCFC studies; utility IRP EV load forecasts
reject_reason: bt_feasibility 3 too low; pipeline winners all have bt>=4 with FRED/yfinance data

name: FDA Patent Cliff Year -> Generic ANDA Filing Surge -> Long TEVA/VTRS
thesis: FDA Orange Book patent expiry dates are public. When >\B branded revenue faces expiry in a single year, track ANDA Paragraph IV first-to-file certifications. When filings on top-10 expiring drugs spike >50% YoY, long TEVA/VTRS 6-12 months before launch. Market underprices 180-day first-to-file exclusivity revenue ramp.
causal_chain: FDA Orange Book shows >\B branded revenue facing expiry in 12-24 months (free)
Generic makers file ANDA Paragraph IV first-to-file certifications (public, free)
First-to-file winner gets 180-day exclusivity at 80% discount to brand
Generic captures 40-70% market share within 6 months of launch
TEVA/VTRS see 5-15% revenue growth from cliff drugs
Long when cliff pipeline exceeds \B and their filings target top drugs
source_reference: FDA Orange Book; Hatch-Waxman 180-day exclusivity; IQVIA patent cliff data

name: US Rig Count Decline → Delayed Oil Production Drop → Long Crude 6mo
thesis: Baker Hughes publishes weekly US rig count (free). When active oil rigs drop >20% from trailing 12mo peak, new well completions decline with a 4-6 month lag (DUC inventory buffers initially). Once DUCs deplete, production growth stalls and oil prices firm. Long CL=F or XOP 6 months after rig count crosses -20% threshold. The lag between rigs and production is the edge — the market watches rigs but misprices the production lag.
causal_chain: Baker Hughes weekly rig count shows active oil rigs down >20% from 12mo peak
Initially, production holds steady as operators complete DUCs (drilled-but-uncompleted wells)
DUC inventory depletes over 4-6 months — EIA DUC report tracks this (free monthly)
New well completions decline → production growth flattens or declines
Supply tightening supports oil prices → CL=F / XOP rally over following 6 months
data_sources: Baker Hughes rig count (free weekly via FRED RIGS or bakerhughes.com)
source_reference: Baker Hughes; EIA Drilling Productivity Report; DUC inventory analysis

name: US Auto Inventory Days-Supply Trough → Long Auto Dealers
thesis: BEA publishes monthly auto inventory-to-sales ratio (FRED AISRSA). When dealer inventory days-supply hits a cyclical trough (<40 days vs 60-day normal), auto dealers have maximum pricing power — no discounts, fat per-unit margins. Long auto dealer stocks (AN Autonation, PAG Penske, LAD Lithia, ABG Asbury) for 6-9 months as elevated margins flow through to earnings. The trough is the signal, not the recovery.
causal_chain: FRED AISRSA (auto inventory/sales ratio) hits cyclical trough — days-supply drops below 40
Dealers have no inventory to discount — buyer demand exceeds available vehicles
Per-unit gross profit spikes ($3-5k above normal) as dealers charge MSRP+ with addendum stickers
Elevated per-unit margins flow through to dealer earnings with 1-2 quarter lag
Auto dealer stocks (AN, PAG, LAD, ABG) re-rate on earnings beats
data_sources: yfinance AN, PAG, LAD, ABG, SPY
source_reference: BEA Motor Vehicle Inventory; NADA dealer profitability surveys; Cox Automotive inventory tracker

name: FDIC Problem Bank List Growth → Short Regional Bank ETF
thesis: FDIC publishes the Quarterly Banking Profile (free) which includes the number of 'problem banks' (CAMELS 4 or 5 rated). The actual names are confidential but the COUNT is public. When problem bank count rises >20% QoQ from a low base (<60), it signals brewing stress in the regional banking sector before specific names blow up. Short KRE (regional bank ETF) when the count inflects upward. Historical: problem banks rose from 50 (2007) to 305 (2009) — the count was the leading indicator.
causal_chain: FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile reports problem bank count (free, fdic.gov/analysis/quarterly-banking-profile)
Count rises >20% QoQ from base <60 — signals stress building below the surface
Individual bank failures start appearing 2-4 quarters after the count inflects (SVB/FRC pattern was visible in 2022 count uptick)
Market contagion fear spreads to regional bank sector → KRE sells off
Short KRE captures the sector-wide risk repricing before specific names are identified
source_reference: FDIC QBP; CAMELS rating system; historical problem bank data back to 1990
reject_reason: Short-side thesis (short KRE). Pipeline pattern: 0/8 short-side backtests have won. Macro overwhelms.

name: US Apartment Construction Completions Surge → Short Apartment REITs
thesis: Census Bureau publishes monthly multifamily completions (FRED COMPU5MUSA). The 2024-2026 apartment supply wave is the largest since the 1980s. When 5+ unit completions spike >30% YoY, the new supply crushes rent growth and occupancy in Sun Belt markets. Short apartment REITs (CPT, MAA, INVH) which are most exposed to the oversupplied markets. This is the inverse of the AL-5 thesis — supply overwhelms demand.
causal_chain: FRED COMPU5MUSA shows 5+ unit completions spike >30% YoY
New apartment deliveries concentrate in Sun Belt metros (Austin, Phoenix, Atlanta, Nashville)
Landlords compete on concessions (1-2 months free rent) to fill new units
Same-store rent growth decelerates or goes negative for Sun Belt REITs
CPT/MAA/INVH guide down NOI → stock re-rates on lower growth trajectory
data_sources: yfinance CPT, MAA, INVH, VNQ, SPY
source_reference: Census Bureau multifamily completions; RealPage supply tracker; NMHC apartment conditions survey
reject_reason: Short-side thesis (short apartment REITs). AL-5 Sunbelt REIT thesis already failed. Same direction.

name: ISM Manufacturing New Orders/Inventories Spread → Industrial Production Lead
thesis: The ISM Manufacturing PMI has a lesser-known internal spread: New Orders minus Inventories. When this spread exceeds +10 (strong orders, lean inventories), industrial production accelerates 2-3 months later with high reliability. Long XLI (industrials ETF) or specific cyclical names (CAT, DE, EMR) for 90 days. The spread is published same day as headline PMI but markets focus on the headline, not the internals.
causal_chain: ISM Manufacturing report: compute New Orders index minus Inventories index
Spread crosses above +10 (strong demand signal with lean inventory = restocking imminent)
Manufacturers ramp production to rebuild inventory → industrial output accelerates in 2-3 months
FRED INDPRO confirms production upturn with 6-week lag
Industrial stocks (XLI, CAT, DE, EMR) re-rate on improving order books + production
data_sources: FRED NAPMNOI (ISM New Orders) + FRED NAPMII (ISM Inventories) — both free monthly
source_reference: ISM Manufacturing PMI; Federal Reserve INDPRO; Koenig 1998 Dallas Fed working paper on ISM internals

name: NOAA Seasonal Drought Outlook → Long Water Utility/Infrastructure
thesis: NOAA CPC publishes a Seasonal Drought Outlook every month (free). When the outlook shows 'drought development likely' or 'drought persistence likely' across 3+ major Western states, water utility and infrastructure stocks rally as municipalities accelerate capital spending on water conservation, recycling, and desalination. Long AWK, WMS, XYL, WTRG for 6 months. The drought outlook leads actual drought designation by 1-3 months.
causal_chain: NOAA CPC Seasonal Drought Outlook flags 3+ Western states for drought development/persistence
State water agencies activate conservation mandates and emergency procurement
Municipal water utilities (AWK) file for rate increases to fund drought-response capex
Water infrastructure suppliers (XYL pumps, WMS drainage, WTRG treatment) see order pipeline grow
Stocks re-rate over 3-6 months as capex commitments flow to earnings
data_sources: NOAA CPC Seasonal Drought Outlook (free monthly at cpc.ncep.noaa.gov)
yfinance AWK, WMS, XYL, WTRG, SPY
source_reference: NOAA CPC; US Drought Monitor; American Water Works investor presentations on drought capex

name: US Treasury Auction Tail → Bond Market Stress Signal → Long VIX
thesis: Treasury auctions (10Y and 30Y) sometimes 'tail' — clearing yield comes in above the when-issued yield by >2bps. A tail signals weak demand from primary dealers and indirect bidders. When 10Y or 30Y auctions tail by >3bps, it predicts higher volatility and equity weakness over the next 2-4 weeks as the funding stress propagates. Long VIX calls or VIXY for 20 trading days. Treasury Direct publishes results within minutes of the auction (free).
causal_chain: Treasury auction clears with a 'tail' — high yield > when-issued by >3bps (TreasuryDirect, free)
Tail signals primary dealers forced to absorb more than expected → balance sheet stress
Dealer hedging (shorting Treasuries, buying vol) pushes rates higher and VIX up
Equity market reprices higher discount rates over 1-4 weeks
VIX spikes 10-20% from pre-auction level within 2 weeks of a tailed auction
source_reference: TreasuryDirect; dealer positioning research (Barclays, JPM); 2023 Oct 30Y tail of 5.3bps preceded equity selloff

name: USGS Earthquake Swarm Near Semiconductor Fab → Short TSM/INTC
thesis: USGS publishes real-time earthquake data (free API). Taiwan sits on the Pacific Ring of Fire; TSMC fabs are concentrated in Hsinchu/Tainan. When a M5.5+ earthquake strikes within 100km of a major fab complex, production pauses for 24-72 hours for tool recalibration (costing ~$50-100M per day in lost output). Short TSM on the event, hold 5-10 days until production-resumption guidance. The USGS alert leads financial news by 5-30 minutes.
causal_chain: USGS earthquake.usgs.gov API reports M5.5+ event within 100km of Hsinchu or Tainan
TSMC activates emergency shutdown protocol — tools require recalibration (24-72h minimum)
Wafer-in-process (WIP) damaged — 1-3 days of production lost = $50-100M revenue impact
TSMC issues production update within 24-48h; sell-side cuts near-term estimates
TSM drops 2-5% over 5 days; recovers over 2-4 weeks as production normalizes
data_sources: USGS earthquake API (free, real-time at earthquake.usgs.gov/fdsnws/event/1/)
source_reference: USGS FDSN API; TSMC earthquake response protocol (2024 Hualien quake case study)
reject_reason: Short-side thesis. Pipeline pattern: 0/8+ short-side wins.

name: Fed Reverse Repo Facility Drain → Equity Liquidity Tailwind
thesis: The Fed's ON RRP facility peaked at $2.6T in Dec 2022 and has been draining steadily. When ON RRP drops below $500B (approaching zero), it signals that money market funds are moving cash INTO the banking system and risk assets rather than parking at the Fed. This is a structural liquidity tailwind for equities. Long SPY when ON RRP crosses below $500B; the excess reserves flow into bank deposits → lending → risk asset demand. FRED series RRPONTSYD tracks this daily (free).
causal_chain: FRED RRPONTSYD shows ON RRP balance dropping below $500B
Money market funds shift from Fed overnight facility to T-bills, bank deposits, and repo
Bank reserves and deposit base expand → credit conditions ease at the margin
Excess liquidity flows into risk assets (equities, credit, crypto)
SPY benefits from structural bid as the liquidity transmission channel reopens
source_reference: NY Fed ON RRP data; J7 Fed RRP drain signal (existing, regime-specific) — this is a LEVEL-threshold variant

name: US New Home Sales Cancellation Rate Spike → Short Homebuilders
thesis: Major homebuilders (LEN, DHI, TOL, KBH) report cancellation rates in quarterly earnings (free SEC filings). When cancellation rates spike >20% (vs 10-12% normal), it signals demand is cracking before it shows in headline orders. The cancellation disclosure leads the next month's Census new home sales report by 4-6 weeks. Short XHB or individual builders for 60 days when cancellation rates spike.
causal_chain: Homebuilder 10-Q/earnings call discloses cancellation rate >20% (free, SEC EDGAR)
Cancellations lead Census new home sales data by 4-6 weeks — Census counts contracts, not completions
Rising cancellations mean locked mortgage rates expired or buyers lost financing confidence
Net orders disappoint next quarter → backlog declines → revenue guidance cut
XHB / individual builders sell off over 60 days as forward earnings estimates compress
data_sources: yfinance LEN, DHI, TOL, KBH, XHB, SPY
source_reference: Lennar/DHI/Toll 10-Q disclosures; 2022 cancellation spike case study (LEN ~26%, DHI ~24%)
reject_reason: Short-side thesis. Pipeline pattern: 0/8+ short-side wins.

name: NOAA El Niño Watch → Long Natural Gas (Winter Demand Disruption)
thesis: El Niño winters bring warmer-than-normal temperatures to the US Northeast and Midwest, reducing heating demand. Conversely, La Niña winters bring colder temperatures, spiking natural gas demand. NOAA CPC publishes ENSO advisory monthly (free). When La Niña Watch is issued by July: long NG=F from Oct 1 for the winter heating season. When El Niño Watch: short NG=F or stay flat. The advisory leads the winter by 3-5 months — enough time to position before weather derivatives price it.
causal_chain: NOAA CPC issues La Niña Watch/Advisory by July (free at cpc.ncep.noaa.gov/products/analysis_monitoring/enso_advisory)
La Niña statistical association: colder US winters, especially Northeast/Midwest
Higher heating degree days → increased residential/commercial natural gas demand
EIA weekly storage draws exceed 5-year average → NG=F rallies through winter
Long NG=F from Oct 1 captures the winter demand premium (hold through Mar 31)
source_reference: NOAA CPC ENSO diagnostic; EIA winter fuels outlook; ENSO-winter temperature correlations (Halpert & Ropelewski 1992)

name: US Existing Home Sales Months-Supply Inversion → Long Homebuilders
thesis: NAR publishes monthly existing home sales with months-supply of inventory (FRED MSACSR). When months-supply drops below 3.0 (extreme seller's market, vs 5-6 balanced), new construction is the only relief valve. Homebuilders (LEN, DHI, NVR) gain pricing power and market share. Long XHB or homebuilder basket for 12 months when MSACSR crosses below 3.0. The signal fires rarely but has led multi-year homebuilder outperformance.
causal_chain: FRED MSACSR (monthly supply of existing homes) crosses below 3.0 months
Extreme inventory scarcity — buyers have no existing-home options
Demand shifts to new construction as the only available supply
Homebuilders (LEN, DHI, NVR) gain pricing power — can raise ASPs + reduce incentives
Gross margins expand → earnings beats → homebuilder stocks re-rate over 6-12 months
data_sources: yfinance LEN, DHI, NVR, XHB, SPY
source_reference: NAR existing home sales; FRED MSACSR; homebuilder earnings correlation with inventory scarcity

name: US Semiconductor Equipment Book-to-Bill Crossing 1.0 → Long SMH
thesis: SEMI publishes monthly North America semiconductor equipment book-to-bill ratio (free summary). When B2B crosses above 1.0 from below (orders exceeding shipments for the first time in a downcycle), it signals the capex recovery inflection. Long SMH for 6 months. The crossing from below 1.0 to above has preceded every semi upcycle since 1990. Markets react to the headline number but underestimate the momentum that follows the 1.0 crossing.
causal_chain: SEMI monthly B2B ratio crosses above 1.0 from below (free summary via semi.org press release)
Orders > shipments = fabs are placing new equipment orders for first time in downcycle
Equipment backlog rebuilds → lead times extend → capacity expansion confirmed
Semiconductor revenue inflects 1-2 quarters later as new capacity comes online
SMH rallies 20-40% over 6 months as the upcycle broadens from equipment to chipmakers
data_sources: yfinance SMH, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, SPY
source_reference: SEMI; semiconductor cycle research (IC Insights, VLSI Research)

name: US Conference Board LEI 6-Month Rate-of-Change Inflection → Long SPY
thesis: The Conference Board Leading Economic Index (FRED LEI, USSLIND) is a composite of 10 leading indicators. When the 6-month rate-of-change turns positive after being negative for 6+ months, it has preceded every economic expansion since 1960. Long SPY for 12 months from the inflection. The signal fires late in recessions (after the worst is over) but early enough to capture the recovery rally. FRED data goes back to 1959.
causal_chain: FRED USSLIND (Conference Board LEI) 6-month annualized rate-of-change turns positive
After 6+ consecutive months of negative readings — confirms the trough, not a false start
LEI composite captures: yield curve, ISM new orders, building permits, jobless claims, stock prices
Positive inflection = majority of leading indicators now pointing to expansion
SPY rallies 15-30% over the following 12 months as earnings recover
source_reference: Conference Board; Estrella-Mishkin 1998; NBER business cycle dating

name: USDA Weekly Crop Progress Planting Pace Delay → Long Corn Futures
thesis: USDA publishes weekly Crop Progress showing corn planting pace as % complete vs 5-year average (free). When corn planting is >15pp behind the 5-year avg by May 15, delayed emergence reduces growing season days, increasing yield risk. Long ZC=F from May 15 through Jun 30. Historically, severely delayed planting years (2019: only 49% planted by May 19 vs 80% avg) produced corn rallies of 10-20% by late June as the market prices in yield penalty.
causal_chain: USDA Crop Progress Monday report shows corn planting >15pp behind 5-year avg by May 15
Wet/cold spring prevents field access — planting window narrows
Late-planted corn has fewer growing degree days → yield penalty of 5-15%
USDA June Acreage report may also show prevent-plant acres reducing supply further
ZC=F rallies 10-20% May-June as market reprices yield expectations downward
source_reference: USDA NASS Crop Progress; Emerson Nafziger (U of I) planting date yield research

name: USDA Cold Storage Butter/Cheese Surplus → Short Dairy Futures
thesis: USDA publishes monthly Cold Storage report (free, FRED series). When combined butter + cheese stocks exceed the 5-year avg by >15%, it signals overproduction that the market hasn't fully priced. Dairy processors (cheese/butter manufacturers) dump excess inventory at discount, pushing cash prices down. Short DC=F (Class III milk futures) or cheese futures for 60-90 days. The Cold Storage report publishes ~22nd of each month — the date is known but the data surprises.
causal_chain: USDA Cold Storage report shows butter+cheese stocks >15% above 5yr avg (free monthly)
Excess inventory signals overproduction relative to demand
Processors begin liquidating surplus at discounted prices to free cold storage space
Cash cheese/butter prices decline, pulling Class III milk futures (DC=F) lower
Short DC=F for 60-90 days captures the inventory drawdown pricing pressure
data_sources: USDA Cold Storage (free monthly, also FRED DSCWBUS for cheese)
yfinance — DC=F may not be on yfinance; check FRED DCHE for cheese price proxy
source_reference: USDA NASS Cold Storage report; CME cheese/butter spot; dairy industry analysts
reject_reason: Short-side thesis. Pipeline pattern: 0/8+ short-side wins.

name: US Dollar Index 3-Month Spike → EM Equity Reversal (Overreaction Fade)
thesis: When DXY spikes >8% in a rolling 3-month window, it creates forced selling in EM equities (margin calls, portfolio rebalancing, FX-hedging costs). The selloff typically overshoots fundamentals. Long EEM (EM ETF) 30 days after the DXY spike peaks (confirmed by DXY rolling over from its 3mo high). The reversal captures mean reversion as forced selling exhausts and EM currencies stabilize. FRED DTWEXBGS (trade-weighted dollar index, broad) provides clean daily data back to 2006.
causal_chain: FRED DTWEXBGS or yfinance DX-Y.NYB shows DXY 3-month return >+8%
Strong dollar triggers forced EM equity selling (margin calls, fund outflows, FX-hedge costs)
EM equities (EEM) overshoot to the downside — selling exceeds fundamental impairment
Wait 30 days for DXY to roll over (confirmation that spike is peaking)
Long EEM for 90 days to capture the mean-reversion recovery
source_reference: DXY-EM correlation research; forced-selling/overreaction literature

name: ASCE Infrastructure Report Card Grade Change → Long Infrastructure ETF
thesis: The American Society of Civil Engineers publishes a national infrastructure Report Card every 4 years (free). When the overall grade worsens (e.g., D+ to D), it generates bipartisan political pressure for infrastructure spending. The 2021 ASCE D+ grade directly led to the $1.2T IIJA passage. Long PAVE (Global X US Infrastructure ETF) or specific contractors (PWR, MTZ, ACM) for 12 months after a worsened grade publication. The next report card is due 2025 — if the grade improves for the first time due to IIJA, the trade inverts (long construction continues on spending confirmation).
causal_chain: ASCE publishes quadrennial Report Card grading US infrastructure A-F (free at infrastructurereportcard.org)
Grade worsens → media coverage → bipartisan political pressure for spending bills
Congress passes infrastructure authorization (IIJA 2021 precedent)
Federal infrastructure spending flows to contractors (PWR, MTZ, ACM, PRIM) over 12-24 months
Infrastructure stocks re-rate on backlog growth + multi-year revenue visibility
data_sources: yfinance PAVE, PWR, MTZ, ACM, SPY
source_reference: ASCE infrastructurereportcard.org; IIJA enactment Nov 2021
reject_reason: bt_feasibility 3 too low; pipeline winners all have bt>=4 with FRED/yfinance data

name: US Commercial Real Estate Transaction Volume Collapse → Long Distressed Buyers
thesis: RCA/MSCI publishes monthly CRE transaction volume (free summary via trade press). When quarterly transaction volume drops >50% YoY, it signals a price discovery freeze — sellers refuse to accept marked-down prices, buyers wait. 6-12 months later, distressed sellers capitulate, transaction volume rebounds, and opportunistic buyers (BX, ARES, BAM, KKR alts arms) deploy dry powder at steep discounts. Long alternative asset managers for 12 months from the transaction volume trough.
causal_chain: CRE quarterly transaction volume drops >50% YoY (MSCI RCA, free summary)
Price discovery freezes — bid-ask spread too wide for deals to clear
6-12 months later: distressed sellers (overleveraged owners, maturing CMBS) forced to transact
Alternative managers (BX, ARES, BAM) deploy record dry powder at 20-40% discounts to peak
Fee income + AUM growth accelerates → alt manager stocks re-rate over 12 months
data_sources: MSCI RCA transaction volume (free quarterly summary)
yfinance BX, ARES, BAM, KKR, SPY
source_reference: MSCI Real Capital Analytics; Preqin dry powder data; GFC/COVID CRE cycle precedents

name: USDA Prospective Plantings Acreage Surprise → Corn/Soy Spread
thesis: USDA publishes Prospective Plantings on March 31 each year (free). This is the most market-moving USDA report for grains. When corn planted acreage exceeds expectations by >2M acres AND soybean acreage misses by >2M acres, the corn/soy price ratio shifts: corn weakens (more supply) and soybeans strengthen (less supply). Trade the spread: long ZS=F / short ZC=F for 30 days. The report drops at noon ET on March 31 — the gap is the entry.
causal_chain: USDA Prospective Plantings published March 31 noon ET (free at usda.library.cornell.edu)
Corn acreage +2M above trade estimate AND soybean acreage -2M below estimate
More corn acres = more corn supply expected at harvest → ZC=F weakens
Fewer soybean acres = tighter soy supply → ZS=F strengthens
Long ZS=F / short ZC=F spread for 30 days captures the rebalancing
source_reference: USDA NASS Prospective Plantings; Irwin/Sanders UIUC acreage surprise research

name: Fed Senior Loan Officer Survey Tightening → Credit Cycle Turn
thesis: The Fed SLOOS (Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey) is published quarterly (FRED). When net % of banks tightening standards for C&I loans exceeds +40% (major tightening), credit contraction follows with 2-3 quarter lag. Long TLT / short HYG (high yield credit) as a pair for 9 months. The SLOOS has preceded every credit cycle downturn since 1990. FRED series DRTSCILM tracks this directly.
causal_chain: FRED DRTSCILM (SLOOS net tightening) crosses above +40%
Banks restricting lending to businesses — credit supply contracting
Corporate borrowers with variable-rate or maturing debt face refinancing squeeze
High-yield credit spreads widen as default risk reprices (HYG declines)
Fed eventually cuts rates in response → TLT rallies
Long TLT / short HYG pair captures both legs
source_reference: Fed SLOOS; K4 SLOOS signal (existing — but that uses 'willingness to lend' not tightening, different series)

name: US Corporate Bond Maturity Wall → Long Short-Duration Credit vs Long-Duration
thesis: Bloomberg/ICE tracks corporate bond maturities by year. When the maturity wall for the next 12 months exceeds $800B (top quartile historically), companies with maturing long-duration debt must refinance at higher rates. Short LQD (investment-grade long-duration) vs long VCSH (short-duration IG) as the maturity wall reprices credit risk for longer-duration issuers. The maturity schedule is published but the equity/credit market underestimates the refinancing cost drag on earnings for specific issuers.
causal_chain: ICE/Bloomberg maturity wall data shows >$800B IG+HY bonds maturing in next 12 months
Companies with maturing bonds must refinance at current rates (often 200-400bps above original coupon)
Long-duration bond issuers face the largest coupon step-up (10-30yr bonds maturing at 3% refinanced at 6%)
Interest expense increases compress EPS for affected issuers over following year
LQD (long-duration IG) underperforms VCSH (short-duration IG) as duration risk reprices
source_reference: ICE BofA US Corporate Index; Fed Financial Stability Report maturity wall analysis

name: US Railroad Traffic Weekly YoY Turn → Industrial Production Lead
thesis: AAR publishes weekly railroad traffic (carloads + intermodal, free). When total carloads YoY turns positive after 12+ weeks of negative readings, it has historically preceded industrial production (FRED INDPRO) positive inflection by 1-2 months. Long XLI (industrials) or specific cyclicals (CAT, DE, GE) for 90 days from the carload YoY turn. Distinct from existing N3/R-N3 (which trades intermodal vs carloads RATIO, not the absolute YoY turn).
causal_chain: AAR weekly rail traffic: total carloads turn positive YoY after 12+ consecutive negative weeks
Carloads = physical goods movement (coal, grain, chemicals, autos, lumber) — direct production proxy
Positive turn signals industrial demand recovery — goods are physically moving again
FRED INDPRO confirms 1-2 months later as factory output data catches up
Industrial stocks (XLI, CAT, DE) re-rate on improving order books and production
data_sources: AAR weekly rail traffic (free at aar.org)
source_reference: AAR weekly rail traffic; distinct from N3/R-N3 intermodal ratio signal

name: USDA Milk Production Monthly Surprise → Dairy Futures
thesis: USDA publishes monthly Milk Production report (free, ~18th of each month). When actual milk production YoY change misses expectations by >1% (less milk than expected), Class III milk futures (DC=F) rally as the market reprices supply tightness. The report is the single most market-moving event for dairy futures. FRED has monthly milk production data (DAIRYPRIMUS) going back decades.
causal_chain: USDA Milk Production report shows YoY change below trade expectations by >1%
Fewer cows or lower per-cow output signals structural tightening (not just seasonal)
Cheese/butter manufacturers face tighter raw milk supply
Class III (cheese) milk futures rally 5-10% over 2-4 weeks as cash market adjusts
If consecutive misses, the move compounds as forward supply estimates are cut
data_sources: yfinance — DC=F may not be available; use DBA dairy component or FRED cash price
source_reference: USDA NASS Milk Production; CME Class III milk futures
reject_reason: bt_feasibility 3 too low; pipeline winners all have bt>=4 with FRED/yfinance data

name: US Bankruptcy Filings Monthly Surge → Long Restructuring Advisors
thesis: ABI (American Bankruptcy Institute) publishes monthly total commercial bankruptcy filings (free summary). When commercial filings rise >30% YoY for 3+ months, restructuring advisory firms see fee revenue surge with a 2-3 quarter lag (engagements take 3-6 months from filing to peak fee generation). Long Lazard (LAZ, restructuring division), Houlihan Lokey (HLI, #1 restructuring bank), PJT Partners (PJT). The bankruptcy count leads restructuring revenue because firms engage advisors at or shortly after filing.
causal_chain: ABI monthly commercial bankruptcy filings rise >30% YoY for 3+ months (free summary at abi.org)
Chapter 11 filings = corporate distress → demand for restructuring advisors, DIP lenders, turnaround consultants
Advisory mandates awarded 1-3 months after filing; fee generation peaks 3-6 months later
HLI, LAZ restructuring, PJT see quarterly revenue acceleration 2-3 quarters after filing surge
Stocks re-rate on earnings beats + expanding backlog of restructuring mandates
data_sources: ABI monthly filings (free summary at abi.org)
FRED TNBCBSL (total nonfarm business bankruptcy, quarterly, free)
source_reference: American Bankruptcy Institute; Houlihan Lokey restructuring revenue disclosures

name: FRED Financial Stress Index Spike → Long Gold / Short High-Beta
thesis: St. Louis Fed publishes the Financial Stress Index weekly (FRED STLFSI4). When STLFSI4 crosses above +1.0 (stressed territory), gold outperforms high-beta equities by 8-15% over the following 60 days as the flight-to-quality trade dominates. Long GLD / short IWM (Russell 2000 = highest-beta equity index) for 60 days. The FSI aggregates 18 financial market variables — it fires less often than VIX but is a broader measure of systemic stress.
causal_chain: FRED STLFSI4 crosses above +1.0 (weekly, free)
Broad financial stress confirmed — credit spreads, vol, equity, funding all deteriorating
Flight to quality: institutional capital moves from risk assets to gold/treasuries
High-beta equities (IWM small caps) sell off hardest — most sensitive to credit/liquidity tightening
Gold rallies as safe haven + central bank response expectations build
Long GLD / short IWM pair captures the divergence over 60 days
source_reference: St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index; existing AB-10 STLFSI4 signal uses a DIFFERENT implementation (equity reduction, not gold/IWM pair)

name: FRED Real Retail Sales YoY Negative → Consumer Discretionary Underperformance
thesis: FRED publishes monthly real retail sales (RRSFS, deflated by CPI). When YoY real retail sales turns negative for the first time in a cycle, it confirms the consumer is rolling over before GDP does. Short XLY (consumer discretionary) vs long XLP (consumer staples) for 6 months. Real retail sales has led consumer spending contraction in every recession. The monthly data leads quarterly GDP consumer component by 1-2 months.
causal_chain: FRED RRSFS (real retail sales) YoY turns negative after 12+ months positive
Consumers are spending less in real terms — purchasing power eroding
Discretionary spending (electronics, restaurants, apparel) hit first; staples resilient
XLY earnings estimates get cut 1-2 quarters later; XLP holds steady
XLY underperforms XLP by 10-20% over the following 6 months
source_reference: Census Bureau retail sales; BEA PCE; consumer spending cycle research

name: US Housing Starts Surge → Long Lumber 3-6 Month Lag
thesis: FRED HOUST (housing starts) leads lumber demand by 3-6 months — starts signal the foundation phase, but lumber is consumed during framing (month 2-4 of construction). When housing starts spike >15% YoY for 2+ months, long lumber (LBS=F or WOOD ETF) for 6 months capturing the framing demand wave. Distinct from PL005 (which uses permits/completions ratio → aggregates); this is starts → lumber, a different material in a different construction phase.
causal_chain: FRED HOUST (monthly housing starts) rises >15% YoY for 2+ consecutive months
New housing starts = foundations poured; lumber demand comes during framing 2-4 months later
Lumber mills ramp but capacity adjusts slowly (shift changes, log inventory cycles)
Demand surge exceeds supply response → lumber spot prices rally 15-30%
WOOD ETF or LBS=F captures the framing-demand wave
source_reference: Census Bureau housing starts; NAHB construction timeline; Random Lengths lumber

name: US Natural Gas Storage Deficit → Long NG=F Before Winter
thesis: EIA publishes weekly natural gas storage (FRED NGTRSTUS). When storage enters October >10% below the 5-year average, the market underprices winter heating risk. Long NG=F from Oct 1 through Jan 31. Storage deficit + normal winter = price spike; storage deficit + cold winter = massive spike. The weekly storage report is the most watched energy data after the petroleum report, but the OCT deficit level as a seasonal entry signal is under-exploited.
causal_chain: FRED NGTRSTUS or EIA weekly storage shows Oct 1 inventory >10% below 5yr average
Below-normal storage entering winter = less cushion for heating demand
Even a normal winter draws storage faster → prices rise to incentivize conservation
Cold winter (La Niña) on top of deficit = price spike 30-60%
NG=F rallies Oct-Jan as market reprices winter supply risk
source_reference: EIA natural gas storage; Gelber & Associates winter storage analysis

name: US Durable Goods New Orders 3-Month Surge → Long Industrials
thesis: FRED DGORDER (durable goods new orders) is published monthly. When durable goods orders rise >10% from their trailing 6-month average for 3 consecutive months, it signals a capex cycle inflection — manufacturers are placing orders for equipment, vehicles, aircraft. Long XLI or specific industrials (CAT, HON, GE, RTX) for 6 months. Durable goods orders lead industrial production by 2-3 months and lead earnings revisions by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED DGORDER (monthly durable goods new orders) rises >10% above trailing 6mo avg for 3mo
Surge signals corporate capex cycle turning — new orders = future production revenue
Manufacturers (CAT, HON, GE) see backlog growth that takes 2-4 quarters to deliver
Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates 2-3 months later confirming demand
Earnings revisions turn positive → XLI/industrials re-rate over 3-6 months
data_sources: yfinance XLI, CAT, HON, GE, SPY
source_reference: Census Bureau durable goods; Conference Board LEI (durable goods is a component)

name: US Used Car Price Decline → Lower CPI → Long TLT (Disinflation Trade)
thesis: FRED CUSR0000SETA02 (CPI used cars and trucks) has direct weight in CPI (~3.5%). Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index (free monthly) leads CPI used cars by 1-2 months. When Manheim drops >5% from 3-month peak, CPI used cars will follow down, pulling headline CPI lower. Long TLT for 60-90 days as disinflation expectations build. Used cars were a major inflation driver in 2021-2022 and their normalization is a major deflation driver now — the symmetry is under-traded.
causal_chain: Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index drops >5% from trailing 3-month peak (free monthly)
Manheim leads CPI used cars by 1-2 months (BLS methodology lags dealer auction prices)
CPI used cars component declines, pulling headline CPI 0.1-0.2pp lower per month
Bond market prices in disinflation → breakevens fall → nominal yields decline
TLT rallies as rate-cut expectations firm on lower inflation prints
data_sources: Manheim Used Vehicle Index (free monthly summary via Cox Automotive)
source_reference: Cox Automotive Manheim Index; BLS CPI methodology (used cars weight ~3.5%)

name: US Construction Spending YoY Surge → Long Heavy Equipment (CAT/DE/URI)
thesis: FRED TTLCONS (total construction spending, monthly) captures both residential and nonresidential construction. When total construction spending rises >10% YoY for 3+ months, heavy equipment demand accelerates with a 1-2 quarter lag — contractors need excavators, loaders, cranes. Long CAT+DE+URI (United Rentals) for 6 months. URI is the purest play (100% equipment rental, volume grows with construction activity). Data back to 2002.
causal_chain: FRED TTLCONS (monthly construction spending) rises >10% YoY for 3+ consecutive months
Sustained construction growth = active jobsites expanding = more equipment needed
Contractors order/rent heavy equipment (excavators, loaders, cranes) with 1-2 quarter lag
CAT sees order books grow, DE construction division ramps, URI rental utilization rises
Equipment stocks re-rate on improving utilization/order metrics over 3-6 months
source_reference: Census Bureau construction spending; CAT dealer inventory reports; URI fleet utilization

name: FRED M2 Money Supply YoY Acceleration → Long Gold 6mo
thesis: FRED M2SL (M2 money supply, monthly) is a broad liquidity measure. When M2 YoY growth reaccelerates above +5% after a period of <+3% (or negative), it signals liquidity expansion that historically flows into hard assets with a 6-12 month lag. Long GLD for 12 months from the reacceleration crossing. M2 growth was negative in 2022-2023 (first time since 1930s); the reacceleration in 2024-2025 should push gold higher as excess liquidity seeks inflation hedges.
causal_chain: FRED M2SL YoY growth crosses above +5% after 6+ months below +3%
Monetary expansion = more dollars chasing same real assets
Excess liquidity initially flows to bonds/equities, then to hard assets with lag
Gold reprices as inflation expectations and debasement fears build
GLD rallies 10-25% over the following 12 months
source_reference: FRED M2; monetary inflation → gold literature (Howell, Tavi Costa); P6 Howell M2/BTC existing but for CRYPTO not gold

name: US New Export Orders ISM Sub-Index Surge → Long Industrials with Export Revenue
thesis: FRED NAPMEI (ISM Manufacturing New Export Orders sub-index) specifically tracks foreign demand for US goods. When NAPMEI crosses above 55 after being below 50 for 4+ months, it signals global demand recovery for US-manufactured products. Long industrials with >30% international revenue (HON, EMR, ITW, PH) for 6 months. NAPMEI is a purer export demand signal than headline PMI, which blends domestic + export orders.
causal_chain: FRED NAPMEI (ISM New Export Orders) crosses above 55 from below 50 (4+ months under)
Foreign demand for US manufactured goods recovering — weaker USD or global growth upturn
US exporters (HON, EMR, ITW, PH) see order books fill from international customers
Revenue acceleration with positive FX translation effects on earnings
Industrial exporters re-rate over 3-6 months as earnings revisions turn positive
data_sources: yfinance HON, EMR, ITW, PH, SPY
source_reference: ISM Manufacturing PMI sub-indices; industrial company 10-K geographic revenue disclosures

name: FRED Bank Credit YoY Reacceleration → Long Financials
thesis: FRED TOTBKCR (total bank credit, weekly) tracks aggregate bank lending. When bank credit YoY growth turns positive after 6+ months of deceleration/contraction, it signals the credit cycle trough. Banks earn net interest income on new loans — credit growth = NIM expansion = earnings growth. Long XLF or specific money-center banks (JPM, BAC, WFC) for 12 months from the reacceleration.
causal_chain: FRED TOTBKCR weekly bank credit YoY growth turns positive after 6+ months below 2%
Credit expansion = banks making new loans, NIM improving on wider spreads
Loan growth drives net interest income — the largest revenue line for banks
Provision releases as credit quality stabilizes — double earnings tailwind
XLF/bank stocks re-rate over 6-12 months as NIM + credit growth compound
data_sources: yfinance XLF, JPM, BAC, WFC, SPY
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.8 Assets and Liabilities of Commercial Banks

name: US Vehicle Miles Traveled YoY Surge → Long Auto Parts Retailers
thesis: FHWA publishes monthly Vehicle Miles Traveled (FRED TRFVOLUSM227NFWA). When VMT YoY growth exceeds +3% for 3+ months, it signals sustained driving demand that translates to tire/parts/maintenance revenue for auto parts retailers (AZO, ORLY, AAP). More miles = more wear = more replacement parts. Long AZO+ORLY for 6 months. VMT leads auto parts same-store sales by 1-2 quarters because wear accumulates before the customer visits the store.
causal_chain: FRED TRFVOLUSM227NFWA (monthly VMT) YoY growth > +3% for 3 consecutive months
More driving = more tire wear, oil changes, brake pad replacements, battery failures
Demand for replacement parts accelerates with 1-2 quarter lag (wear accumulates before repair)
AZO and ORLY (80%+ DIY/DIFM auto parts) see same-store sales accelerate
Stocks re-rate on earnings beats as parts demand flows through to financials
source_reference: FHWA Traffic Volume Trends; AutoZone/O'Reilly investor presentations on VMT correlation

name: FRED Senior Loan Officer Survey Easing → Long Small Caps (IWM)
thesis: FRED SUBLPDRCSC (SLOOS net % of banks easing standards for small firms) is the mirror of the tightening signal (PL050). When banks shift from tightening to easing for small firm C&I loans, it's the credit-cycle trough — small businesses regain access to credit. Small-cap stocks (IWM) are the most credit-sensitive equity segment because small companies depend on bank lending, not bond markets. Long IWM for 12 months from the easing inflection. SLOOS easing has preceded every small-cap recovery since 1990.
causal_chain: FRED SUBLPDRCSC (SLOOS small-firm easing) shifts from net-tightening to net-easing
Banks loosening credit standards for small businesses — credit cycle troughing
Small companies regain access to working capital, inventory financing, capex loans
Small-cap revenue/earnings recovery accelerates 2-3 quarters later
IWM rallies 20-40% over 12 months as small-cap earnings recover
source_reference: Fed SLOOS; small-cap credit sensitivity literature; distinct from PL050 (which trades TLT on tightening, not IWM on easing)

name: US Copper Imports Surge → Infrastructure Spending Confirmation → Long Steel/Infra
thesis: Census Bureau publishes monthly trade data with commodity detail (FRED IMP0690 or custom Census API). When US copper imports surge >15% YoY for 3+ months, it signals domestic infrastructure/construction activity accelerating — copper is consumed in wiring, plumbing, electrical systems. This is a physical demand confirmation signal. Long infrastructure beneficiaries (PWR, MTZ, VMC, NUE) for 6 months. Copper imports lead construction spending by 1-2 months because copper is ordered early in the project lifecycle.
causal_chain: Census monthly trade data shows US copper imports YoY > +15% for 3 months
Rising copper imports = physical infrastructure/construction demand accelerating domestically
Copper consumed in wiring, plumbing, electrical — ordered early in construction projects
Infrastructure contractors (PWR, MTZ) and materials (VMC, NUE) see revenue growth 1-2 quarters later
Stocks re-rate as quarterly results confirm the demand flow-through
data_sources: Census Bureau trade data (free monthly, also via FRED foreign trade series)
yfinance PWR, MTZ, VMC, NUE, SPY
source_reference: Census Bureau FT900; copper demand as infrastructure leading indicator

name: US Trucking Tonnage YoY Positive After Decline → Long Transports
thesis: ATA publishes monthly For-Hire Truck Tonnage Index (FRED TRUCKD11). When tonnage YoY turns positive after 6+ months of negative readings, it signals goods-economy recovery. Long IYT (transport ETF) or specific truckers/rails (UNP, NSC, ODFL) for 6 months. Tonnage is a physical goods measure that leads GDP goods-component by 1-2 months. Distinct from PL052 (AAR carloads/INDPRO proxy) — this is TRUCK tonnage specifically.
causal_chain: FRED TRUCKD11 (ATA truck tonnage) YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative
Truck tonnage = physical goods being shipped on highways — direct demand proxy
Positive turn = goods economy recovery confirmed (inventory restocking underway)
Transport stocks (IYT, UNP, ODFL) see volume and pricing recovery
Re-rate over 3-6 months as freight rate recovery follows volume
source_reference: ATA For-Hire Truck Tonnage; distinct from PL052 (AAR rail carloads) and AR-3 (Cass freight index)
